class.name: RiveControl
class.brief_description: Plays a Rive artboard as a UI control with pointer interaction.
class.description: Renders [member file]'s artboard (contain-fitted into the control rect, resize-aware) through the Rive Renderer on the GPU, and forwards mouse input into artboard space — Rive listeners (hover, click, drag) work with no code. Standard [member Control.mouse_filter] semantics apply. State machine inputs appear in the inspector under [code]inputs/[/code] and view-model properties under [code]data_binding/[/code] — both are real properties, so AnimationPlayer tracks and Tweens work on them. Requires a RenderingDevice rendering method (Forward+/Mobile); on unsupported renderers the file still loads and the state machine still advances, but nothing draws.

method.name: set_bool_input
method.description: Sets a boolean state machine input.

method.name: set_number_input
method.description: Sets a number state machine input.

method.name: fire_trigger
method.description: Fires a trigger state machine input.

method.name: set_property
method.description: Writes a view-model property. Dispatch is by value type: [bool], [int]/[float], [String] (also enums), [Color], or [Image]/[Texture2D] for image properties. Paths may traverse nested view models: [code]"stats/health"[/code].

method.name: get_property
method.description: Last value seen for a watched property (see [method watch_property]); falls back to the last value set.

method.name: watch_property
method.description: Starts reporting changes of a view-model property via [signal property_changed]. The current value is reported immediately (except triggers, which only report when fired).

method.name: fire_property_trigger
method.description: Fires a view-model trigger property.

method.name: set_artboard_property
method.description: Binds the named artboard from the same file to an artboard view-model property.

method.name: replace_view_model
method.description: Swaps the nested view-model instance at [param path] for a new instance of [param view_model] (a fresh instance when [param instance_name] is empty).

method.name: list_append
method.description: Appends an instance of [param view_model] to a list property. Watch the list path to observe its size.

method.name: list_remove_at
method.description: Removes the list item at [param index].

method.name: list_swap
method.description: Swaps two list items.

method.name: list_clear
method.description: Removes all list items.

method.name: list_set_property
method.description: Writes a scalar property on the list item at [param index].

method.name: on_event
method.description: Calls [param callable]([code]properties: Dictionary[/code]) whenever the Rive event [param name] fires. Returns the connected wrapper — pass it to [code]disconnect("rive_event", wrapper)[/code] to unsubscribe.

method.name: on_property
method.description: Watches [param path] and calls [param callable]([code]value[/code]) on every change (the path is auto-watched). Returns the connected wrapper for [code]disconnect("property_changed", wrapper)[/code].

method.name: list_read_property
method.description: Asynchronously reads a list item's scalar: the value arrives via [signal property_changed] at the synthetic path [code]"path[index]/sub_path"[/code] and is cached for [method get_property].

method.name: send_pointer_event
method.description: Injects a pointer event in control-local coordinates (same path as [method Control._gui_input]). Phases: [code]0[/code] move, [code]1[/code] down, [code]2[/code] up, [code]3[/code] exit. [param pointer_id] enables multitouch (one id per finger).

method.name: focus_next_element
method.description: Moves Rive's internal keyboard focus to the next focusable element (shows the focus ring).

method.name: focus_previous_element
method.description: Moves Rive's internal keyboard focus to the previous focusable element.

method.name: send_text_input
method.description: Sends text to the focused Rive element (text fields). Printable keys arriving through [method Control._gui_input] are forwarded automatically.

method.name: submit_gamepad_batch
method.description: Power-user/testing entry: submits a raw Rive gamepad wire-v2 batch. Normal use is [member gamepad_enabled], which encodes Godot's gamepad state automatically each frame.

method.name: get_semantics_node_count
method.description: Number of semantic (accessibility) nodes currently mirrored from the artboard. Useful for tests; requires [member accessibility_enabled].

members.member: The [code].riv[/code] file to play. Changing the resource (hot reload) rebuilds the instance in place.
Artboard name (empty = the file's default). Shown as a dropdown in the inspector.
State machine name (empty = the artboard's first).
Whether the state machine advances each frame.
Stop advancing while not visible in the tree.
Playback speed multiplier (also affected by [member Engine.time_scale]).

How the artboard maps to the texture: [code]0[/code] Contain (default), [code]1[/code] Cover, [code]2[/code] Fill, [code]3[/code] Fit Width, [code]4[/code] Fit Height, [code]5[/code] None, [code]6[/code] Scale Down, [code]7[/code] Layout. [b]Layout[/b] resizes the artboard itself so its Rive layout (Yoga) reflows to the node's size — use it for responsive UI. Pointer input and hit testing stay accurate in every mode.
Anchor for fit modes that do not fill exactly, as a 9-grid index (0 = top-left … 4 = center … 8 = bottom-right).

Content scale for [code]fit = Layout[/code]: the artboard is laid out at [code]size / layout_scale[/code] and drawn scaled up. Constant physical size: set to [code]DisplayServer.screen_get_dpi() / reference_dpi[/code].
When non-empty, this node's Rive audio plays through a dedicated engine into an internal [AudioStreamPlayer] on the named bus. Empty (default) mixes into the shared global [RiveAudioStream].
When enabled, Godot gamepad state is diffed and forwarded to Rive every frame (W3C-standard mapping), driving Rive's gamepad listeners and focus navigation.

[code]0[/code] Opaque (default): the whole rect captures pointer events. [code]1[/code] Translucent: only points over an interactive Rive listener capture — clicks over empty or decorative regions fall through to controls behind (listener-aware, not alpha-based: invisible hit areas still capture).

Mirrors the artboard's authored semantic tree (roles, labels, bounds) into Godot's accessibility system (AccessKit) as sub-elements of this control, making Rive UI visible to screen readers. Elements are published while assistive technology is active.

signal.name: rive_event
signal.description: A Rive event reported by the state machine, with its custom properties.

signal.name: state_changed
signal.description: The state machine entered a new animation state.

signal.name: property_changed
signal.description: A watched view-model property changed (see [method watch_property]).

signal.name: loaded
signal.description: Emitted when the (asynchronously created) Rive instance becomes live — property reads and pointer input are meaningful from here. Property writes made earlier are cached and replayed automatically. Re-fires after every recreate (file/artboard/fit changes).
